questions.q: In which Indian film industry was the actress Suchitra Sen primarily a leading star?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen was the foremost leading lady of Bengali cinema, often called the First Lady of Tollywood.

questions.q: With which legendary Bengali actor did Suchitra Sen form one of Indian cinema's most celebrated on-screen romantic pairings?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen and Uttam Kumar formed the iconic romantic pairing of Bengali cinema across dozens of films.

questions.q: What was the birth name of the actress known as Suchitra Sen?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen was born Roma Dasgupta in 1931 before adopting her screen name.

questions.q: In which decade did Suchitra Sen begin her film career?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen began her acting career in the early 1950s, becoming a star with the 1953 film Saare Chuattar.

questions.q: In which year did Suchitra Sen pass away?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen died on 17 January 2014 in Kolkata at the age of 82.

questions.q: In the 1955 Hindi film Devdas, opposite Dilip Kumar, which character did Suchitra Sen portray?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen played Paro (Parvati), the childhood love of Devdas, in Bimal Roy's 1955 Hindi film Devdas.

questions.q: In which 1975 Hindi political drama did Suchitra Sen play a woman politician widely seen as inspired by a real prime minister?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen starred as politician Aarti Devi in Gulzar's 1975 film Aandhi, opposite Sanjeev Kumar.

questions.q: Suchitra Sen is most associated with films made in which Indian language?
questions.explanation: Though she acted in several Hindi films, Suchitra Sen's primary body of work was in Bengali cinema.

questions.q: Which honorific nickname is commonly used to describe Suchitra Sen's status in Bengali cinema?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen is widely celebrated as the First Lady of Tollywood, the Bengali film industry.

questions.q: Suchitra Sen's daughter, who also became a well-known actress, is named:
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen's daughter Moon Moon Sen became an actress and later a politician.

questions.q: For her role in which film did Suchitra Sen win the Best Actress award at the 1963 Moscow International Film Festival?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen won Best Actress at the 1963 Moscow Film Festival for the Bengali film Saat Paake Bandha.

questions.q: What distinction did Suchitra Sen achieve by winning at the 1963 Moscow International Film Festival?
options: First Indian to win an Academy Award
First woman to direct an Indian film
First Indian actress to win an international acting award
First Indian to win at the Cannes Film Festival
questions.explanation: She became the first Indian actress to win an international acting award with her Moscow Film Festival prize.

questions.q: In which year was the Bengali romantic film Saptapadi, pairing Suchitra Sen with Uttam Kumar, released?
questions.explanation: Saptapadi, one of the most famous Sen-Kumar films, was released in 1961.

questions.q: The 1957 Bengali film Harano Sur, a celebrated Suchitra Sen and Uttam Kumar romance, revolves around which plot element?
options: A patient with amnesia and the doctor who loves him
questions.explanation: In Harano Sur, Suchitra Sen plays a doctor who falls in love with an amnesiac patient played by Uttam Kumar.

questions.q: Which 1959 Bengali film starred Suchitra Sen as a troubled woman in a psychiatric drama directed by Asit Sen?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen starred in the 1959 psychological drama Deep Jwele Jaai, later remade in Hindi as Khamoshi.

questions.q: In the 1975 film Aandhi, who played Suchitra Sen's estranged husband, a hotel manager?
questions.explanation: Sanjeev Kumar played J.K., the hotel manager and estranged husband of Sen's politician character in Aandhi.

questions.q: Which renowned filmmaker directed the 1975 Suchitra Sen film Aandhi?
questions.explanation: Aandhi (1975) was written and directed by Gulzar, with music by R. D. Burman.

questions.q: In which year was the Bimal Roy film Devdas, featuring Suchitra Sen as Paro, released?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy's Devdas, starring Dilip Kumar with Suchitra Sen as Paro, was released in 1955.

questions.q: Which national civilian honour, awarded by the Government of India, did Suchitra Sen receive in 2012?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen was conferred the Padma Shri by the Government of India in 2012; the Banga Bibhushan she received the same year was a West Bengal state award, not a national one.

questions.q: After retiring from films, Suchitra Sen became known for which aspect of her later life?
options: Living in seclusion away from public view
questions.explanation: After retiring around 1978, Suchitra Sen withdrew into reclusive seclusion, rarely appearing in public for decades.

questions.q: Suchitra Sen's first released Bengali film as a leading actress, in 1953, was:
questions.explanation: Saare Chuattar (1953) marked Suchitra Sen's first major released film and notably paired her with Uttam Kumar.

questions.q: The 1954 Bengali hit Agni Pariksha, a major success for Suchitra Sen, co-starred which actor?
questions.explanation: Agni Pariksha (1954) was a landmark Sen-Uttam Kumar romance that cemented their on-screen partnership.

questions.q: In which 1960 Hindi film did Suchitra Sen star opposite Dev Anand?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen appeared opposite Dev Anand in the 1960 Hindi film Bombai Ka Babu.

questions.q: Suchitra Sen's character Aarti Devi in Aandhi (1975) was widely perceived as resembling which real-life political figure?
questions.explanation: The character's appearance and persona led many to associate it with Prime Minister Indira Gandhi, contributing to the film's temporary ban during the Emergency.

questions.q: In which 1960 Bengali film, a major Suchitra Sen and Uttam Kumar romance, did Sen play a doctor's daughter named Rina Brown?
questions.explanation: In Saptapadi (1961), Suchitra Sen played the Anglo-Indian character Rina Brown opposite Uttam Kumar's Krishnendu.

questions.q: Suchitra Sen reportedly declined which of India's highest film honours in 2005, citing her reclusive lifestyle?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen reportedly declined the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 2005 because accepting it would have required a public appearance in Delhi.

questions.q: In which year was the Bengali film Saat Paake Bandha, which earned Suchitra Sen her Moscow Festival prize, released?
questions.explanation: Saat Paake Bandha was released in 1963, the same year Suchitra Sen won Best Actress at Moscow for it.

questions.q: Most of Suchitra Sen's films were set against the social and cultural backdrop of which Indian region?
questions.explanation: As a Bengali cinema icon, Suchitra Sen's films were predominantly rooted in Bengali social and cultural settings.

questions.q: Suchitra Sen made her final film appearance in 1978 in which Bengali film?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen's last screen appearance was in the 1978 Bengali film Pranay Pasha, after which she retired from cinema.

questions.q: In which town, in present-day Bangladesh, was Suchitra Sen (born Roma Dasgupta) born and raised before moving to Calcutta?
questions.explanation: Suchitra Sen was born and raised in Pabna, in present-day Bangladesh, before settling in Calcutta after her marriage.
